## Perspective Audit Summary **Component/artifact reviewed:** Infinite Scroll Feed With Cognitive Navigation Breakdown **Perspectives escalated for deep review:** Keyboard Motor, Screen Reader Semantic, Cognitive Neurodivergent **Perspectives at LOW (not reviewed):** Magnification, Environmental Contrast, Vestibular, Auditory --- ### Findings by Severity | Severity | Count | |---|---| | CRITICAL | 0 | | MAJOR | 3 | | MINOR | 1 | | ENHANCEMENT | 0 | --- ### Perspectives That Passed - None (all escalated perspectives have findings) --- ### Perspectives With Findings — Grouped by ARRM Role **Front-End Dev** - **Dynamic content focus management missing** — Keyboard Motor — MAJOR — 2.4.11 Focus Not Obstructed - **Missing pause option for infinite scroll** — Cognitive Neurodivergent — MAJOR — 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide - **Redundant aria-label on article container** — Screen Reader Semantic — MINOR — 1.3.1 Info and Relationships **UX Design** - **Layout shifts during infinite scroll** — Cognitive Neurodivergent — MAJOR — 3.2.3 Consistent Navigation --- ### Open Questions Requiring Human Verification - None (all findings are code-level) --- ### Recommended Next Step **REVISE** (MAJOR findings present — address before shipping) --- ## Detailed Findings ``` **Finding:** Dynamic content loading breaks keyboard navigation flow — focus remains at bottom of viewport after new cards appear **WCAG:** 2.4.11 Focus Not Obstructed **Perspective:** Keyboard Motor **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: UX Design) **Evidence:** New cards appended to DOM without focus management; Tab key remains at last focusable element before load **Fix:** Add `containerRef.current.scrollTop = containerRef.current.scrollHeight` after appending new cards to maintain scroll position ``` ``` **Finding:** Infinite scroll lacks pause/stop mechanism for users with vestibular or cognitive needs **WCAG:** 2.2.2 Pause, Stop, Hide **Perspective:** Cognitive Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Front-End Dev) **Evidence:** No option to disable automatic content loading; sentinel triggers load on intersection **Fix:** Add "Pause auto-load" button in UI with `aria-live` announcement and `aria-pressed` state ``` ``` **Finding:** Layout shifts during content loading create disorientation for neurodivergent users **WCAG:** 3.2.3 Consistent Navigation **Perspective:** Cognitive Neurodivergent **Severity:** MAJOR **Route to:** UX Design (secondary: Visual Design) **Evidence:** New cards appear at bottom of feed without visual cue, causing viewport to jump **Fix:** Add subtle animation or border highlight to new cards with `transition: opacity 0.3s` and `transform: translateY(-10px)` ``` ``` **Finding:** Redundant aria-label on article container duplicates heading content **WCAG:** 1.3.1 Info and Relationships **Perspective:** Screen Reader Semantic **Severity:** MINOR **Route to:** Front-End Dev (secondary: Content Author) **Evidence:** `aria-label={card.title}` on `
` duplicates `

` content **Fix:** Remove `aria-label` since heading already provides sufficient context ``` --- ## Code-Level Evidence 1. **Focus management gap** `setCards(prev => [...prev, ...generateCards(prev.length + 1, 10)])` No focus adjustment after DOM update 2. **Missing pause mechanism** `const observer = new IntersectionObserver(...)` No user control over sentinel activation 3. **Redundant ARIA** `
` Duplicates `

{card.title}

` 4. **Layout shift** New cards appended to `.feed-container` without transition `setCards(prev => [...prev, ...generateCards(...)])` --- ## Severity Calibration Rationale - **MAJOR (2.4.11, 2.2.2, 3.2.3):** Disrupts core navigation patterns for keyboard and neurodivergent users, requires architectural changes - **MINOR (1.3.1):** Redundant ARIA doesn't block access but creates unnecessary verbosity for screen readers --- ## Recommended Fixes 1. **Keyboard Motor (Front-End Dev):** Add `useEffect` to adjust scroll position after load: ```js useEffect(() => { if (!loading) containerRef.current.scrollTop = containerRef.current.scrollHeight }, [loading]) ``` 2. **Cognitive Neurodivergent (UX Design):** Add pause control: ```jsx ``` 3. **Cognitive Neurodivergent (Visual Design):** Add transition to new cards: ```css .feed-card { transition: opacity 0.3s, transform 0.3s; } .feed-card.new { opacity: 0; transform: translateY(-10px); } ``` 4. **Screen Reader Semantic (Front-End Dev):** Remove redundant `aria-label`: ```jsx
``` --- ## A11y Evidence Finding finding_id: infinite-scroll-focus-gap fingerprint: 3a7d4e2b source: perspective-audit Keyboard Motor checklist wcag_or_apg: 2.4.11 Focus Not Obstructed section_508_fpc_context: 1194.22(j) severity: MAJOR perspective_alarms: keyboard_motor=HIGH, screen_reader_semantic=MEDIUM, cognitive_neurodivergent=HIGH evidence: New cards appended to DOM without focus management (line 25) reproduction_steps: Tab to last card, trigger sentinel load expected_behavior: Focus should return to top of new content area actual_behavior: Focus remains at bottom of viewport trend: new
